title: Anacamptis × larzacensis
taxonomy: Anacamptis × larzacensis is a hybrid orchid species, classified as a natural hybrid between Anacamptis pyramidalis and Anacamptis palustris. This classification is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics, which are intermediate between its parent species. As a hybrid, Anacamptis × larzacensis is not assigned a rank in the botanical classification system, but it is often referred to as a subspecies or a variety. The exact classification of Anacamptis × larzacensis can vary depending on the source and the taxonomic approach used. Further research and analysis are needed to fully understand the classification of this hybrid species.

intro: Anacamptis × larzacensis, also known as the Larzac Orchid, is a hybrid orchid species native to the Larzac region of France. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis.
